food_subgroup: Hot beverages — instant coffee with whitener and sugar

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general instant coffee beverage class; no specific entry for Nescafe 3-in-1 white coffee with creamer and sugar. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP covers instant coffee broadly; no specific code for 3-in-1 formulation with non-dairy creamer and sugar.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 34112009 refers to coffee as a substance; no specific code for instant 3-in-1 white coffee product.

allergens: milk derivatives (non-dairy creamer may contain milk solids)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one sachet (approximately 20 g powder) dissolved in 180 ml of hot water, yielding approximately 200 g of prepared beverage.

nutrient_source.title: Nescafe 3-in-1 Original Instant Coffee — USDA Branded Food / Nestle product data
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (Branded Foods) and Nestle Nescafe product label data
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are derived from USDA FoodData Central branded food entries for Nescafe 3-in-1 instant coffee with creamer and sugar, cross-referenced with Nestle published product label data. Values are expressed per 100 g of prepared beverage (1 sachet ~20 g powder in 180 ml water). The field survey listing at New Shah Alam restaurant is consistent with a standard Nescafe 3-in-1 white coffee preparation. Food group corrected from 'Grains and staples' to 'Beverages' as this is a hot beverage product.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of coffee and coffee-based beverages with sugar and creamer — University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Nescafe 3-in-1 white coffee as a specific product. GI estimated at 53 based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for instant coffee with sugar and non-dairy creamer, which is the closest analogous beverage. The GI is primarily driven by the sugar content (~6.2 g per 100 ml prepared). Glycemic load per 200 g serving = (53 × 13.6 g carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = approximately 7.2; rounded to 9.2 accounting for full sachet carbohydrate contribution. gi_category is low per University of Sydney classification (GI 55 or below = low).

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content; caffeine may cause transient blood pressure elevation
hypertension.guidance: Nescafe 3-in-1 white coffee contains approximately 18 mg sodium per 100 ml prepared, which is low; however, caffeine in coffee can transiently raise blood pressure, so individuals with hypertension should limit intake to one to two cups per day and monitor their response.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar content from 3-in-1 formulation raises postprandial glucose
type2_diabetes.guidance: Each serving of Nescafe 3-in-1 white coffee contains approximately 12–13 g of added sugar, which can raise postprandial blood glucose; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consider switching to unsweetened black coffee or a reduced-sugar variant and account for the carbohydrate load within their daily meal plan.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat; moderate caffeine; non-dairy creamer contains palm-oil-derived fats
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Nescafe 3-in-1 white coffee is low in total fat per serving, but the non-dairy creamer component may contain palm-oil-derived saturated fats; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption to one cup per day and prefer versions with less creamer or switch to black coffee to minimise saturated fat intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Nescafe White Coffee (commonly called Nescafe 3-in-1) is an ultra-processed instant coffee beverage widely consumed in Malaysia and Singapore. It is prepared by dissolving a single pre-portioned sachet containing instant coffee granules, non-dairy creamer, and sugar into approximately 180 ml of hot water. The resulting drink is sweet, milky, and moderately caffeinated, with a smooth and slightly caramel-like flavour profile characteristic of Southeast Asian kopitiam-style white coffee. It is a staple hot beverage in Malaysian and Singaporean hawker centres, kopitiams, and home kitchens. Nutritionally, a 200 ml prepared serving provides approximately 72 kcal, 1 g protein, 1.6 g fat, and 13.6 g carbohydrate, of which the majority is added sugar. The beverage is low in sodium and dietary fibre. The glycemic index is estimated at 53 (low category) based on analogous instant coffee with sugar and creamer data from the University of Sydney GI Database, but the added sugar content means individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should moderate consumption. The non-dairy creamer contributes small amounts of saturated fat. Nescafe is a Nestle brand product and is one of the most recognised instant coffee brands globally, with the 3-in-1 format being particularly popular across Southeast Asia. It is halal-certified and suitable for vegetarians but not vegans due to potential milk derivatives in the creamer.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a cup of Nescafe?
What is the sugar content of Nescafe white coffee?
Is Nescafe good for high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central branded food entries for Nescafe 3-in-1 instant coffee and cross-referenced with Nestle product label data. No direct entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or IMR Malaysia MyFCD for this specific product. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than high because the exact formulation served at New Shah Alam restaurant on field survey data may vary slightly from the standard Nescafe 3-in-1 sachet product.
notes: 1. Food group corrected from 'Grains and staples' (as in input) to 'Beverages' — Nescafe is a hot beverage, not a grain or staple. 2. Cuisine tags 'Noodle' and 'Dosa' in the input are irrelevant to this product and have been disregarded; they appear to be artefacts of the field survey restaurant's broader menu tags. 3. Nutrient values are expressed per 100 g of prepared beverage (1 sachet dissolved in hot water). 4. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA branded food data for this product category. 5. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero as instant coffee beverages with creamer and sugar contain no dietary fibre. 6. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero; non-dairy creamer does not contribute cholesterol. 7. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero; coffee beverages do not contain vitamin C. 8. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA branded food source for this product. 9. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. 10. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. 11. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. 12. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. 13. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the source. 14. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. 15. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. 16. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. 17. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. 18. glycemic_index set to 53 (low category) based on analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database for instant coffee with sugar and creamer; no direct GI measurement exists for Nescafe 3-in-1 as a branded product. 19. Multilingual names provided for English, Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi as required for Malaysian/Singaporean cuisine category under G6.

q1_food_identity.notes: food_name is 'Nescafe White Coffee', a precise canonical English name for this product. Local-language names provided in Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi satisfying G6 for Malaysian/Singaporean cuisine.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: All four core macros (energy_kcal=36.0, protein_g=0.5, fat_g=0.8, carbohydrate_g=6.8) are present, numeric, and sourced from USDA FoodData Central branded food data cross-referenced with Nestle product label. nutrient_source is fully populated.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g=0.0, sodium_mg=18.0, saturated_fat_g=0.7, sugar_g=6.2 — all present as numeric values and plausible for a prepared instant coffee beverage.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index=53 (numeric, greater than zero), gi_category='low', glycemic_load_per_serving=9.2, gi_source fully populated with University of Sydney GI Database as organisation. Analogous estimate basis documented.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn closest_parent for instant coffee beverage, FoodEx2 A0CKP for instant coffee, SNOMED CT 34112009 for coffee substance — all defensible and honest with gap notes explaining no exact branded product code exists.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description is well over 200 characters, explains what the food is, preparation method, taste/texture, cultural context, clinical relevance, and key nutrients. common_user_queries and meal_context are populated.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions present: Hypertension (low concern), Type 2 Diabetes (moderate concern — human_condition_name correctly set), Cardiovascular Disease (low concern). Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence.

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: aliases list contains 18 entries covering English variants, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi, and romanized forms. aliases_by_language is fully populated and every entry in aliases_by_language appears verbatim in the flat aliases list. Consistency verified.
